title: Talking Transports: Autumn Transport’s Focus on Bulk Freight

In this episode of the Talking Transports podcast, Autumn Transport CEO Julie Andrich joins Bloomberg Intelligence senior transportation and logistics analyst Lee Klaskow to discuss how the company’s focus on food, feed, agricultural and industrial bulk freight has insulated it from much of the volatility affecting traditional truckload markets. title: Oil Falls to Three-Month Low as US-Iran Deal Set to Add Supply
summary: Oil headed for its longest run of declines this year as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts and key market gauges tumbling.

title: Stanford's Wallace Says US Recession Is Not a Certainty
summary: Stanford Management Company CEO Rob Wallace discusses his views on the US economy, and the challenges of managing a $50 billion portfolio for Stanford University's endowment. He joins David Rubenstein on this week's episode of Bloomberg Wealth. The interview was recorded April 9 in Palo Alto, CA.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
summary: Binance Holdings Ltd. is preparing to stop providing its services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

title: S&P 500 Slips as Semiconductor Stocks Take a Breather; SpaceX Extends Gains
summary: US stocks fell Tuesday, led lower by declines for technology companies after recent big gains.

title: JPMorgan Markets Debt for Long Lake’s $6.3 Billion Amex GBT Deal
summary: JP Morgan Chase & Co. has begun marketing a debt package to help fund Long Lake Management Inc’s. $6.3 billion acquisition of Global Business Travel Group Inc., the travel platform spun out of American Express Co., adding to a recent pickup in leveraged-buyout financings coming to market.

title: SpaceX Set to Overtake Amazon in Value as It Soars for Third Day
summary: SpaceX jumped for a third straight day on Tuesday, putting it on track to overtake Amazon.com Inc. in value and briefly making it the world’s fourth largest stock ahead of Microsoft Corp.

title: A Billion Dollars of Leveraged SpaceX Bets Hit ETFs in One Day
summary: Leveraged bets on SpaceX generated more than $1 billion of trading volume on their first day, as investors rushed to amplify wagers on the biggest IPO in history, underscoring the extraordinary retail appetite surrounding Elon Musk’s newest public company.

title: S&P 500 Rally Falters Before Fed as SpaceX Surges: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally that sent stocks to the brink of all-time highs faltered on the eve of the first Federal Reserve rate decision under Kevin Warsh, with a another slide in oil prices driving bond yields lower.

title: World Cup Exposes Growing Global Prediction Market Rift
summary: This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.

title: Debt Chiefs Tout Benefits of Hedge Fund Growth in Bond Markets
summary: Some sovereign debt chiefs are warming up to the growing footprint of hedge funds in government bond markets, casting these investors as potentially beneficial participants rather than a source of risk.

title: Wall Street Stares Down Crypto-Style Trading All Day, Every Day
summary: Traditional finance firms are starting to embrace round-the-clock trading for a range of assets in a way that has disrupted schedules in crypto for years.

title: Brightline Bondholders Grant Two-Week Extension on Debt Payment
summary: Brightline Trains Florida LLC, the struggling $6 billion private railroad between Miami and Orlando, struck a deal with holders of $985 million of municipal bonds to extend a debt payment due Monday through the end of the month, according to a securities filing.

title: Wells Fargo Boosts S&P 500 Year-End Target as US-Iran Deal Nears
summary: A combination of easing macroeconomic worries, improving sentiment and interest-rate hike expectations that are priced in will enable US stocks to move higher, according to Wells Fargo Securities LLC.

title: Nvidia’s New Bonds Are Trading Actively After $25 Billion Sale
summary: Initial trading volume has been strong for Nvidia Corp.’s new bonds, though prices have remained near where the $25 billion of debt was sold at.

title: France's Confavreux on Iran Deal, Hormuz, Tech, Digital Tax
summary: French Foreign Ministry spokesman Pascal Confavreux speaks with Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall on the sidelines of the G7 gathering in Evian, France. He discusses the deal with Iran, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, as well as tolls concerns and incentives. He also addresses technology, AI, and France's digital services tax. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Offers Conditional $500 Million Loan for Rare-Earths Plant
summary: Mining startup Phoenix Tailings Inc. received a conditional commitment for $500 million in long-term debt financing from the Pentagon’s Office of Strategic Capital to help build a domestic rare-earth processing facility.

title: Biofuels Are Key to Preventing Sharp Drop in Farmland, S&P Says
summary: Expanding biofuel demand is a “powerful catalyst” to restore profitability to American farms, according to a new study, as harvests balloon and lawmakers consider broadening the market for corn-based ethanol.

title: Essar Signs $500 Million IRH Deal to Buy Oil, Sell Stanlow Fuels
summary: Essar Group’s energy unit has tied up with International Resources Holding’s trading arm to source crude for its Stanlow refinery in the UK and market products in a deal worth $500 million.

title: Gold Advances as Trump Touts Reopening of Hormuz This Week
summary: Gold rose after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen on Friday, a development that would ease an energy and inflation shock that has roiled global markets.

title: SpaceX Options Trading Starts Strong as Traders Chase Musk Mania
summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. options were among the most heavily traded among US companies on Tuesday, helping to spur an early 17% gain in the rocket maker in its third day of trading.

title: Trump Says Iran Deal Includes 99.9% of What He Wants
summary: President Donald Trump says the peace deal with Iran includes "99.9% of what he wants." Speaking at the Group of Seven summit in France, Trump said the deal will prevent Iran from ever having a nuclear weapon. The deal is set to be signed on Friday in Switzerland. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Vanderbilt University to Sell Up to $430 Million of Muni Bonds
summary: Vanderbilt University is planning to sell up to $430 million of tax-exempt bonds to pay for capital projects.

title: Ex-Tory Treasurer Merges Advisory Firm Fenchurch With Broadhaven Capital
summary: Fenchurch Advisory Partners, the London-based advisory boutique focused on the financial services industry, has agreed to merge with Broadhaven Capital Partners to accelerate its expansion into the US market.

title: Yum to Sell Pizza Hut; Dave & Buster’s Sinks | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Yum! Brands (YUM) agreed to sell its struggling Pizza Hut chain for $2.7 billion to a private equity firm and its China business. LongRange Capital will acquire Pizza Hut ex-China for $1.5 billion, Yum said in a statement Tuesday. Yum China Holdings Inc. will buy the rest of the business for $1.2 billion. The transactions are expected to close in the third quarter.

- Dave & Buster’s (PLAY) shares tumble after the operator of restaurant and entertainment facilities reported comparable sales for the first quarter that missed the average analyst estimate. Research firm Benchmark downgraded the stock, citing a lack of confidence that same-store sales will stabilize/eventually turn positive this year. 

- Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares are up after it said it is reducing its full-time employee workforce by 10%, and also closing a small number of open roles. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yum! Will Sell Struggling Pizza Hut for $2.7 Billion
summary: Yum! Brands is selling the chain to private equity firm LongRange Capital. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter. Yum! will now focus more on its better-performing Taco Bell and KFC chains. Michael Halen of Bloomberg Intelligence is on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: CSN Cement Sale Faces Questions as Holcim Resists Huaxin Bid
summary: Huaxin Cement Co.’s bid for the cement unit of Brazil’s Cia. Siderurgia Nacional is facing resistance from one of the Chinese company’s key shareholders, Holcim Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Housing Starts Drop to the Weakest Pace Since 2020
summary: New residential construction in the US slowed to the weakest pace in six years, driven by a steep decline in apartment projects.

title: ECB’s Lane Says Inflation Is in the Pipeline Despite Iran Deal
summary: The European Central Bank must be prepared for inflation that’s been unleashed by the conflict in the Middle East but is yet to be felt, according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

title: Race to Turn AI Compute Into a Commodity Spurs New Crypto Boom
summary: Ethan Vera thought Luxor Technology’s future was helping Bitcoin miners buy and sell mining hardware.
